back_translation: Job continued his discourse, saying:
'As surely as God lives, who has denied me justice, and the Almighty, who has embittered my soul,
as long as my breath is still within me and the breath of God is in my nostrils,
my lips will not speak wickedness, and my tongue will not utter deceit.
I will never admit that you are right; I will hold fast to my integrity until I die.
I will hold tightly to my righteousness and never let go; as long as I live, my conscience will not accuse me.
May my enemy be like the wicked, and my opponent like the unjust.
For what hope does the godless have when he is cut off, when God takes away his life?
Will God hear his cry when distress comes upon him?
Will he delight in the Almighty? Will he call upon God at all times?
I will teach you about the power of God's hand; I will not conceal the ways of the Almighty.
Behold, all of you have seen it for yourselves; why then do you speak such empty nonsense?
This is the wicked man's portion from God, the inheritance the ruthless receive from the Almighty.
Though his sons are many, they are destined for the sword, and his offspring will not have enough food.
His survivors will be buried by plague, and their widows will not weep for them.
Though he heaps up silver like dust and piles up clothing like clay,
what he stores up the righteous will wear, and the innocent will divide his silver.
The house he builds is like a moth's cocoon, like a hut a watchman makes.
He lies down wealthy, but will do so no more; when he opens his eyes, everything is gone.
Terrors overtake him like a flood; in the night a storm sweeps him away.
The east wind carries him away, and he is gone; it sweeps him out of his place.
It hurls itself at him without mercy as he tries with all his might to flee from its power.
It claps its hands at him in mockery and hisses him out of his place.'
